mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and expanding economic activity, which translates into higher consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of PAYEMS consistently correlates with negative S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window, or if the correlation is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above a critical threshold, it signals an increased likelihood of aggressive monetary policy tightening by the Federal Reserve, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where the CPIAUCSL trigger is met, or if FEDFUNDS does not subsequently rise significantly.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and a significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a severe contraction in consumer demand and labor market health, leading to a sharp downturn in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE shows no significant predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, any significant month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a nascent recovery in the labor market, boosting consumer confidence and corporate outlook, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the signal fails to produce positive returns when UNRATE is above 7%, or if the effect is equally strong when UNRATE is below 7% (indicating no regime specificity).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing activity and efficient supply-side capacity utilization, which underpins corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth consistently correlates with negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-year period, or if the correlation is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of housing starts (HOUST) indicates a significant slowdown in the construction sector and related industries, signaling broader economic weakness and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence wanes.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where the HOUST trigger is met, or if the signal's predictive power diminishes significantly during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is actively rising while GDP growth (GDPC1) is simultaneously slowing, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy impacting a weakening economy, severely dampening corporate profitability and leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25 AND GDPC1 2-quarter annualized change < 1%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and GDPC1 shows no significant predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low GDP growth, any positive surprise in GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a potential turning point in the business cycle, leading to increased investor confidence and positive S&P 500 returns as recovery expectations build.
failure_condition: If the signal fails to produce positive returns when GDPC1 growth is below 1%, or if the effect is equally strong when GDPC1 growth is above 1% (indicating no regime specificity).
